quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of namefag listings moved over the board, bringing scattered showers of usernames and biographical details. Anon contributed to the storm, requesting more namefags to be posted.

In other areas, a brief fog of incel anxiety rolled in with a discussion about nudes, but quickly lifted. A weak front of demographic questioning developed, with Anon expressing surprise at the site's userbase not matching their expectations.

Responses came in quickly, dispelling the initial impression. A scattered high-pressure system of tech talk formed, with Anon pondering the future of artificial intelligence and its impact on jobs.

Nearby, a brief drizzle of generational comments occurred, with a claim that the 90s generation is obsessed with the cape genre, sparking a few replies.

articles.title: Anon Lists 'Namefags' on Indiachan /b/
articles.body:
Anon started a thread on Indiachan /b/ by posting 'Namefags of Inch/bhach
Post them'. This prompted others to respond with a list of names.

One anon replied with a list including 'Pajaat saab', 'Ozymandias', 'Brutal bhangi', 'Broken chamar', 'Bengali rad', 'Jee aspirant', 'Indranon', 'Crowanon', and 'bharatiye ex-musalmaan'.

Another anon also provided a list, mentioning 'Haryana Villager', 'Pajaat Saab', 'Benagli Rad', 'Indranon', 'Crowanon', and 'BPA', promising to add more later. A respondent asked for 'lore' about these names, seeking more information.

Another anon inquired if 'bhartiye ex muslim' and 'bi-bottom' referred to the same person. A subsequent response clarified that 'GTB' and 'bi-bottom' were the same individual. Other contributions to the thread included the names 'Dayush' and 'GTB'.

One user, 'Dogra chad', responded with 'Kys pajeet'. The thread appears to be a collection of usernames or nicknames, with some discussion around their possible connections or identities.

The exchange suggests that these names may be associated with particular topics or groups on Indiachan /b/, but further context is not provided. The thread continues to gather information about these names and their significance.

The discussion remains focused on sharing and verifying the names, without diverging into broader topics or explanations.

articles.title: Anon seeks nudes, discussion ensues
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post reading "iske nudes kaha hai?" Another anon responded with a claim that the subject in question was not underage, saying "her body isnt of a child. your dadi probably got deflowered at 16. also she isnt underage. its a lie. she is 19/20".

However, a respondent called out the original poster, writing "Nigga she’s a child. Her brain hasn’t developed fully".

The discussion quickly turned heated, with one participant boasting about their caste status and claiming to have a "janeu" - a sacred thread worn by some high-caste Hindus.

The poster wrote "i am highest caste person in this whole chan, how many of you dalitoid larpers have janeu? none. i have it but, you fucking chamars kys, i am the free-willed brahmin".

Anon also brought up a point about the age of consent and the morality of consuming certain types of content, with one writing "Don't do it pyaaro, she wasn't 18 then.

It is nothing less than committing pedophilia" and another saying "old enough to play with her pussy in front of a camera you moral bhangi".

The thread also included several offensive and extremist statements, including a claim that "if i could i would round 200 million mullas and throw them into blast furnaces where steel is made" and another that called for violence against a user, "Abe dalit pedo, you should be killed".

Some users expressed concern about the content and its implications, with one writing "This site will shutdown if he allows literal cp" and another saying "Why are mods not deleting it. I reported it multiple times".

articles.title: Anon Questions Site's Demographics, Gets Responses
articles.body:
Anon expressed surprise that the site wasn't filled with high-T indra-worshipping masculine Aryans who role-played as liberal/self-loathers for fun, but instead seemed to be full of incel trash projecting their subhumanity on others.

One respondent claimed that looksmaxxing, a topic discussed on the site, is a Jewish psyop targeting men with Caucasoid bones, such as Indians, whites, and Arabs, while ignoring black men.

This anon argued that black men can easily attract women due to Jewish brainwashing, despite not following looksmaxxing.

Another anon countered that the original poster had likely never left their region and was unaware of the realities of black men being prioritized in media.

This respondent claimed that black men are often paired with attractive women on platforms like Netflix, while men with Caucasoid bones have to work hard on looksmaxxing.

A different anon accused the original poster of being a "padav subhuman" who posts caste baits. One respondent suggested that a particular individual had potential but wasted their time creating anti-Hindu threads instead of working on self-improvement.

The discussion also touched on the topic of physical appearance, with one anon stating that being tall is an advantage when coupled with a good face, but not a guarantee of success.

Another respondent mocked an individual's physical characteristics, calling them "uggo" and suggesting that they could hide their appearance with long hair and a beard.

The conversation was marked by several personal attacks and insults, with one anon telling another to "stfu" and calling black men "niggers." The discussion also involved claims about Jewish influence and the effectiveness of looksmaxxing, with some anons arguing that it is a scam or a psyop.

articles.title: Anon ponders tech's future
articles.body:
Anon discussed the future of tech on Indiachan. One poster, Tier 1 cse, claimed friends interning at top tech companies are 'basically babysitting AI to make it do work' and joked that their seniors use AI for code reviews.

Anon responded with suggestions for alternative careers, including apiculture, mushroom farming, and off-season cultivation. The conversation turned to personal financial situations, with Tier 1 cse mentioning a net worth of 2 crore and considering state PCS and CGL preparation.

articles.title: Anon claims 90s generation obsessed with cape genre
articles.body: Anon stated that the 90s generation is obsessed with the cape genre, while zoomers are not interested. Another anon replied, saying both DC and Marvel are created by Jews, and that's why they don't consume comic books. A respondent countered that every comic outside of DC and Marvel is also created by Jews.
